instruction: Leave the bathroom and closet. Exit the bedroom and take a right. Enter the room on the right and stop in the doorway.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.

I'm in a bathroom with a sink and toilet, so I need to look around for a way out and explore the space further.
I can see a doorway ahead leading into a bedroom where there's a bed visible, so I'll move through it.

I see a closet on the right and a window on the left, so I'll keep turning right to get a full view of the room.
I've backtracked to the closet and bathroom area, so I need to retrace my steps and find the actual bedroom exit instead. Let me turn right and continue exploring from there.
I'm hitting a wall here, so let me adjust my approach and turn right to explore a different direction.

I'm getting a better sense of the layout now—there's a chair, a window or glass door in the center, and some furniture like a wardrobe on the right side. I should keep looking for the exit door.

I'm looking at a doorway that opens into a walk-in closet with hanging clothes, and I can see a bathroom sink area further back. Since I've already left the bathroom and closet area according to the instructions, I'm trying to piece together the layout of these connected spaces.

The space has shifted—what I'm seeing now is much larger and emptier than the decorated bedroom, with just a white door partially open on the left and a chair on the right. This feels like a different area entirely, maybe a hallway or adjacent room. I should continue turning to get a fuller picture of where I am before deciding which direction to explore.

I'm tracking my rotations through the space—after 24 right turns from step 57, I should be back to the original orientation, but the views aren't matching up the way I'd expect, so I'm recalculating to figure out where the discrepancy is. So there's a separate walk-in closet within the bedroom itself (with hanging clothes), distinct from the shelving hallway passage that connected the bathroom to the bedroom. The "plain room with door" wall I found is the actual exit—a partially open white door leading out with a chair visible beyond. I need to navigate to that wall and go through it, then turn right as instructed. Since I'm currently facing the bed/chair/TV wall, I can turn right 18 steps to reach the exit door wall directly.
Good, this matches the door I saw before. Let me move toward it.

I'm moving through the doorway into empty rooms ahead.
I'm at the bedroom exit now, facing a hallway with a corridor to my left. The instructions say to turn right after exiting, so I'll head in that direction to see what's there.
I've hit an obstacle—maybe a wall or a closed door. Let me pause and look around to get my bearings.
I'm looking around this room with the bench and window, trying to figure out which way to go to find the right direction through the hallway.

I'm retracing my steps through the space—I turned around in what appears to be the same bedroom and now I'm looking back through the door I came from. The layout is becoming clearer: there's the bedroom behind me, and adjacent to it is another room with a couch and window. Let me work through the exact sequence again, starting from when I was at the door.

After exiting the bedroom, I was in a hallway facing back toward the door. Turning right 90 degrees showed me the couch room, and turning another 90 degrees showed the bedroom directly opposite. This couch room with the window is likely the target—the "room on the right" from the instructions. I should head back to that orientation and move into its doorway.
I'm orienting myself in the couch room again—this is the space to the right when exiting the bedroom. Let me look around to get my bearings and locate the doorway that leads into this room.

I'm recognizing this blank wall as the same view from step 110—the edge of the door frame I came through. I'm recognizing this door as the one I came through from the bedroom earlier, and I can see the landing with stairs and railing beyond it. Let me step back through to get my bearings in the main hallway.
I'm positioned right at the door frame edge, so I'll pivot left to get a view of the landing and the stairs beyond it.

I'm getting a clearer picture of the space now—the bedroom opens onto a landing that connects to a stairwell area with skylights and railings. I should turn right to face that opening directly and see what's beyond.
I'm keeping the same door edge view but adjusting my position slightly—turning left a bit less, maybe three steps, so I can see the landing more directly in front of me.
